result:
Compile is green (warnings only, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added (I used `/tmp/c_slice*.lua` scratch files outside the repo).

## Report

**Root cause:** Two stubs in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` left `break` as a no-op:
- `breakstat` (line 3720) consumed the `break` token but never emitted a JMP or recorded a goto entry, so control fell through into the loop's `until` condition test.
- `leave_block` (line 2798) had a placeholder where it should have called `createlabel(ls, "break", …)` to resolve pending `break` gotos when leaving a loop block.

Together this caused `repeat … break … until x>=12` to evaluate the until condition (false) and loop forever.

**Fix:** Wired `breakstat` to call `cg_jump` and `new_goto_entry` with the interned `b"break"` string. Restructured `leave_block` to snapshot the block fields from `fs.bl` (without taking it out), run `remove_vars`, then call `createlabel(state, b"break", 0, false)` while `fs.bl` still points to the loop block (so `solvegotos` can read `bl.firstgoto`), and only after that pop the block from the chain.

**New state:** `repeat ... break ... until` now exits correctly. `constructs.lua` progresses past line 4 ("testing syntax") and now fails at line 235 with `assertion failed!` — a different downstream bug unrelated to this fix.

tool_input.description: Run with full combined source, show exit code
Run combined test source and capture exit code

tool_input.command: TEST_TIMEOUT_S=5 ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/constructs.lua 2>&1 | tail -5; echo "---wc of out"; wc -l harness/impl/official/constructs.out
tool_input.description: Re-run with shorter timeout to check if it's a hang

tool_input.command: env TEST_TIMEOUT_S=5 ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/constructs.lua 2>&1 | tail -3; wc -l harness/impl/official/constructs.out

tool_input.description: Try a small slice of the test

tool_input.command: sed -n '1,200p' reference/lua-c/testes/constructs.lua > /tmp/c_slice.lua && echo 'print("slice3-end-OK")' >> /tmp/c_slice.lua && ./harness/run_official_test.sh /tmp/c_slice.lua 2>&1 | tail -5; echo "---out tail---"; tail -10 harness/impl/official/c_slice.out
